Tests that the Boolean prototype is not enumerable.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that iterates over the properties of the `Boolean` object in JavaScript. It checks if any property of `Boolean` is equal to `Boolean.prototype`. If such a property is found, the script calls the `assert` function with `false`, which typically indicates a failed assertion in testing contexts. This script provides narrow functionality, specifically for verifying that `Boolean.prototype` is not enumerable as a property of the `Boolean` object.
